observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require:
a. The OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
1. Record purchases of supplies and materials for stock to their appropriate inventory accounts and recognize as expense only when stocks are issued upon receipt of the SSMI with duly validated original copy of the RIS pursuant to Sections 121 of NGAS for LGUs Vol. 1; and
2. Maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
b. The GSO designate to:
1. Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit it immediately, along with the RIS, to the OIC-Municipal Accountant as basis of Journal Entry Voucher to recognize expenses based on the cost of the supplies and materials issued; and
2. Conduct a semestral physical count of inventory items by type, prepare the RPCI, and submit the results to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 and ensure that all Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished, approved and serially numbered;
b. Require the concerned drivers to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels, as well as the designated agency personnel to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption using the prescribed forms under COA Circular No. 77-61 and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to book up as fuel, oil and lubricants expense those that are duly supported by the Monthly Report of Official Travels, Report of Fuel Consumption, with duly filled up Driver’s Trip Tickets.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts and make necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements and furnish the Audit Team the corrections made therein.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) and other concerned departments to formulate specific project/activity, indicating the brief description, location, time of implementation, for every program that partakes the nature of an investment and capital expenditures.
b. Require the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to expedite execution of the planned projects and activities and strictly observe proper timelines to guarantee the timely completion of PPAs;
c. Require the MPDC, in coordination with the MEO, to closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized development PPAs embodied in the LDP and AIP; and
d. Direct Budget Officer to reassess unimplemented projects and projects with unused balance in the 20% Development Fund to address other developmental projects that are needed by the constituents.

We recommended that Management fill up the mandatory MDRRMO position pursuant to Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of R.A. No. 10121, and that the office be composed of a municipal DRRM Officer and three DRRM staffs prescribed in NDRRMC-DIL-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

1.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Prepare the necessary entries to exclude programs/activities that were
incorrectly included in the “Due to NGAs” account;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to examine the unreconciled balances
of the “Due to NGAs” account and exert effort to recover documents that
will identify the nature, specific purpose, source agency, and other
relevant information. Thereafter, facilitate an evaluation to determine
whether the corresponding projects were implemented and return the
unused funds to the source agency, if warranted; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer
to process the refund of the unutilized balances of completed or idle
projects to the concerned Source Agencies in compliance with COA
Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and the applicable
Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to re-calculate the amount of depreciation expense to adjust the carrying amount of Road Networks and account for the change in accounting estimate in accordance with IPSAS 3 - Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors.
We recommended and Management agreed to terminate and utilize these funds for projects that could immediately benefit the constituents.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the SDO to render liquidation and refund any unexpended balance on advances as required under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, thus, resulting in unrecognized expenses in the books and exposes government funds to misuse and misapplication.
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate account Advances for Payroll to record the amount granted to regular disbursing officer for payment of salaries, wages, honoraria, allowances and other personnel benefits for proper recognition of accounts in the financial statements.

observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee was created but did not perform its responsibilities. Moreover, timely submission of  RPCPPE was not done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to issue demand to the Accountable Officers and other persons concerned to liquidate their outstanding cash advances as required under COA Circular No. 97-002 and refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts; and 

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reclassify cash advances granted for travel purposes to Advances to Officers and Employees instead of Due from Officers and Employees pursuant to Annex B- Description of Accounts of COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 prescribing the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: There were liquidations made for unliquidated cash advances  granted in CY 2022 and prior years. However  Advances to SDO amounting to ₱201,310.00 granted in CY 2022 is still unliquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

Record purchases of supplies and materials for stock to their appropriate inventory accounts and recognize as expense only when stocks are issued upon receipt of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) with original copy of the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) pursuant to Sections 121 of NGAS for LGUs Vol. 1; and

Maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 


Require the Supply/ Property Custodian Officer to:

Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit it immediately, along with the RIS, to the OIC-Municipal Accountant as basis of Journal Entry Voucher to recognize expenses based on the cost of the supplies and materials issued; and

Conduct a semestral physical count of inventory items by type, prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), and submit the results to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommend that management:

a.	Direct Acting Municipal Treasurer to initiate and supervise the proper proceedings for the land titling and to cause the immediate transfer of title to the Municipality to ensure absolute ownership and to protect its legal interest pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular 92-386; and 

b.	Direct the Budget Officer to allocate fund/budget for the survey/relocation and titling of untitled lots.

We recommended that management:

Adopt proper controls on the approval and use of government vehicles and the consumption of fuel, oil and lubricants;

Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 77-61 and ensure that all   Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished, approved and serially numbered; and

Require the concerned drivers to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels (Annex F) as well as the designated agency personnel to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption (Annex G) using the prescribed form under COA Circular 77-61 and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management: 

a.	Require the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and Budget Committee to ensure that all identified and prioritized development PPAs funded by 20% DF are investment and capital expenditures in nature, specific, well-planned, and procurement/implementation-ready.

b.	Require the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to expedite execution of projects to guarantee the timely completion; 

Require the MDC, in coordination with the MEO, to closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized development PPAs embodied in the LDP and AIP; and

Direct Budget Officer to reassess unimplemented projects that are of the nature of MOOE and projects with unused balance in the 20% Development Fund to address other developmental projects that are needed by the constituents.

We recommended that Management:

Allocate no less than Direct the Municipal Health Officer and/or concerned RHU personnel to prepare and maintain a Summary/List of donated vaccines received, distributed and balances based on the updated stock cards and furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant, such documents necessary to record the receipt and distribution of vaccines, ancillaries and Supplies Inventory Report or its equivalent in relation to the vaccination program; and 

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record using the perpetual inventory system the vaccines received and its distribution as reported by the Municipality to DOH-CHD, and draw Journal Entry Voucher to record the remaining supplies of vaccines in stock to applicable inventory accounts.

2.6  We recommended and Management agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to re-calculate the amount of depreciation expense to adjust the carrying amount of Road Networks and account for the change in accounting estimate in accordance with IPSAS 3 - Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors.

3.10  We recommended and Management agreed to require:
The OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
Record purchases of supplies and materials for stock to their appropriate inventory accounts and recognize as expense only when stocks are issued upon receipt of the SSMI with duly validated original copy of the RIS pursuant to Sections 121 of NGAS for LGUs Vol. 1; and
Maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
The GSO designate to:
Consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit it immediately, along with the RIS, to the OIC-Municipal Accountant as basis of Journal Entry Voucher to recognize expenses based on the cost of the supplies and materials issued; and
Conduct a semestral physical count of inventory items by type, prepare the RPCI, and submit the results to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
4.8   We recommended and Management agreed to terminate and utilize these funds for projects that could immediately benefit the constituents.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the SDO to render liquidation and refund any unexpended balance on advances as required under COA Circular No. 97-002 and to refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate account Advances for Payroll to record the amount granted to regular disbursing officer for payment of salaries, wages, honoraria, allowances and other personnel benefits for proper recognition of accounts in the financial statements.

6.8   We recommended and Management agreed to:
Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 and ensure that all Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished, approved and serially numbered;
Require the concerned drivers to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels, as well as the designated agency personnel to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption using the prescribed forms under COA Circular No. 77-61 and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to book up as fuel, oil and lubricants expense those that are duly supported by the Monthly Report of Official Travels, Report of Fuel Consumption, with duly filled up Driver’s Trip Tickets.

7.6  We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts and make necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements and furnish the Audit Team the corrections made therein.

8.14  We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) and other concerned departments to formulate specific project/activity, indicating the brief description, location, time of implementation, for every program that partakes the nature of an investment and capital expenditures
Require the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to expedite execution of the planned projects and activities and strictly observe proper timelines to guarantee the timely completion of PPAs;
Require the MPDC, in coordination with the MEO, to closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized development PPAs embodied in the LDP and AIP; and
Direct Budget Officer to reassess unimplemented projects and projects with unused balance in the 20% Development Fund to address other developmental projects that are needed by the constituents.

9.7   We recommended that Management fill up the mandatory MDRRMO position pursuant to Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of R.A. No. 10121, that the office be composed of a municipal DRRM Officer and three DRRM staffs prescribed in NDRRMC-DIL-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

We recommended that Management submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that management:

Stop charges of administrative and operating expenditures to the LDRRMF; and

Ensure that all charges and expenditures in the LDRRMF comply with the allowable expenses as provided under Section A of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and Item 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular 2014-1.

Direct the LCE to properly execute the  full implementation of the 20% development projects and programs as planned during the current year to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated; and

Require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAs) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. Likewise, assistance by other offices be extended to Supply Officer to audit inventory;
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower complement and Covid-19 Pandemic Protocols

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Designate to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the year ending December 31, 2020.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to R.A. No. 10121 otherwise known as “Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Create an  Inventory Committee and come up with a Physical Inventory Plan to facilitate the submission of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any, and
Require the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of PPE, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Exert effort in the processing of Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the LGU pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular 92-386;
Allocate fund intended for the survey/relocation and titling of these lots; and
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper proceedings for the land titling and to cause the immediate transfer to the Municipality to ascertain the ownership.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Direct  the Local Chief Executive to cause the concerned department heads to properly execute the full implementation of the 20% development programs and projects in a timely manner as planned, to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated; and
Strict compliance with Section 287 of RA No. 7160 and Section 5 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1dated February 22, 2017.
Direct LDC to reassess unimplemented, projects that are of the nature of MOOE, projects with unused balance in the 20% Development Fund over the past two (2) years and consider realignment to other urgent priorities to address other developmental projects that are needed by clientele.

We recommended and the management agreed for the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents and the Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls. Likewise, observe timely submission of the CY 2021 Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements as provided in Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 to facilitate timely audit of accounts.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Designate to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the year ending December 31, 2021.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to RA 10121 otherwise known “the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.

We recommended and the management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant  regularly submit on time  statements of bank reconciliation to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445.
Strict compliance of Section 287 of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160 and Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 5 of  DILG/DBM  Joint  Memorandum  Circular  (JMC) No. 2011 dated  April 13, 2011.
observations.reason: Due to unimplemented prior year’s PPAs

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the aforementioned supporting documents to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the BGCM transactions amounting to ₱3,902 million;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to throughly verify each disbursement as to the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents to strengthen internal control in the disbursement process; and

Adhere to the procedures and requirements provided in Section 4 (6) of P.D. No. 1445, Item 9.2.5 of COA  Circular No. 2012-001  dated  June 14, 2012  and  GPPB  Circular  No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 particularly in the conduct of emergency procurement under the Bayanihan Act.
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any; and

We recommended and the Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be required to prepare statements of bank reconciliation and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor regularly within the period prescribed under Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.reason: Submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements are still delayed.

observations.recommendation:
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous work & lack of manpower complement

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Direct the LCE to properly execute the  full implementation of the 20% development projects and programs as planned during the current year to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated; and
Strict compliance of Section 287 of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160 and Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 5 of  DILG/DBM  Joint  Memorandum  Circular  (JMC) No. 2011 dated  April 13, 2011.

We recommended and the Management agreed  to the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the aforementioned supporting documents to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the BGCM transactions amounting to ₱3,902 million;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to throughly verify each disbursement as to the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents to strengthen internal control in the disbursement process; and
Adhere to the procedures and requirements provided in Section 4 (6) of P.D. No.  1445, Item 9.2.5 of COA  Circular No. 2012-001  dated  June 14, 2012  and  GPPB  Circular  No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 particularly in the conduct of emergency procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAs) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. Likewise, assistance by other offices be extended to Supply Officer to audit inventory;
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be required to prepare statements of bank reconciliation and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor regularly within the period prescribed under Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Designate to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the year ending December 31, 2020.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to R.A. No. 10121 otherwise known as “Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.

Improve its collection effort by regularly and properly sending out notices to delinquent taxpayers; and

Enforce collection remedies provided under Sections 256 and 260 of RA No. 7160, particularly the sale of delinquent properties through public auction in order to demonstrate the municipality’s will power and capability to enforce the laws on real property taxation and deter tax evasions.

We recommended that Management require the OIC- Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their cashbook balances and subsidiary ledger balances for Cash Local Treasury (01-01-010),    Cash in Bank (01-02-010/020) and Advances for Payroll at least quarterly to correct detected errors and adjust reconciling items in their respective books/records.

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor regularly within the period prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445.

Request the depository banks to furnish them bank statements/snapshots immediately every end of the month for prompt preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements.
observations.reason: Due to delay in issuance of bank statements.

observations.recommendation:
Stop utilizing the 20% DF for the rehabilitation of Buildings and Other Structures and Infrastructure Assets of various Elementary and High Schools as well as for the improvement of Other Structure projects of the Municipality instead of other priority socio-economic development projects and programs to ensure compliance with the provision of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. 

Funding of programs, projects and activities of public schools should be charged   against SEF.

We recommended  that  Management maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation and regular monitoring of GAD related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided and  see to it that PPAs are in  conformity  with  Section1.2  of  Joint  Circular No. 2004-1  of  DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of  Joint  Memorandum  Circular  No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.

We recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.

We  recommended that management  require the  Municipal Accountant to see to it that  the monthly net take home pay of an employee should not be lower than 5,000.00 before issuing certification to lending institutions in strict compliance to Section 52 of  Republic  Act. No. 10924 also known as General Appropriation Act of 2019.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the monthly net take home pay of an employee should not be lower than 5,000.00 before issuing certification to lending institutions in strict compliance to Section 52 of Republic Act. No. 10924 also known as General Appropriation Act of 2019.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:

a. Improve its collection effort by regularly and properly sending out notices to
delinquent taxpayers; and

b. Enforce collection remedies provided under Sections 256 and 260 of RA No.
7160, particularly the sale of delinquent properties through public auction in
order to demonstrate the municipality’s will power and capability to enforce the
laws on real property taxation and deter tax evasions.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management require the OIC-
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their cashbook
balances and subsidiary ledger balances for Cash Local Treasury (01-01-010),
Cash in Bank (01-02-010/020) and Advances for Payroll at least quarterly to correct
detected errors and adjust reconciling items in their respective books/records.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
1. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation
statements and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor regularly within the
period prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445.
2. Request the depository banks to furnish them bank statements/snapshots
immediately every end of the month for prompt preparation of monthly bank
reconciliation statements.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:

a. Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and
effect necessary adjustments, if any.

b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to immediately
maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and
equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for
LGUs, Volume I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:

a. Exert effort in the processing of Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in
order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the
LGU pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular 92-386;

b. Set aside fund for the titling of these lots and direct the Municipal
Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper payments of expenses
for the land titling and to update and reconcile the inventory of property
assets with the updated records of the OIC-Municipal Accountant.

c. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the subject lots based on
fair market value of the land.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that management:

a. Stop utilizing the 20% DF for the rehabilitation of Buildings and Other Structures
and Infrastructure Assets of various Elementary and High Schools as well as for
the improvement of Other Structure projects of the Municipality instead of other
priority socio-economic development projects and programs to ensure
compliance with the provision of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

b. Funding of programs, projects and activities of public schools should be charged
against SEF.

We recommendeded that Management maximize the utilization of the GAD funds
through the implementation and regular monitoring of GAD related programs,
projects and activities in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided
and see to it that PPAs are in conformity with Section1.2 of Joint Circular No.
2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA so that gender issues are
addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the immediate submission of the Reports
of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents,
Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits
and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to
it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No.
95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that management require the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that the monthly net take home pay of an employee should
not be lower than 5,000.00 before issuing certification to lending institutions in strict
compliance to Section 52 of Republic Act. No. 10924 also known as General
Appropriation Act of 2019.

We recommended that Management require the OIC- Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their cashbook balances and subsidiary ledger balances for Cash Local Treasury (01-01-010), Cash in Bank (01-02-010/020) and Advances for Payroll at least quarterly to correct detected errors and adjust reconciling items in their respective books/records.
observations.reason: Due to lack of coordination and manpower complement in their respective offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be required to prepare statements of bank reconciliation and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor regularly within the period prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445. Management should also hire or assign personnel with Accounting Degree for the Accounting Unit so that delay in the preparation of financial statements and accounting records will be lessened.
observations.reason: Due to delay in issuance of snapshots by the bank.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Committee to submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year; b. Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any. c. Require the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

observations.recommendation: a. Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided and see to it that PPAs are in conformity with Section1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted. b. Proper and regular monitoring of the implementation of GAD programs and projects so that full implementation of the same will be achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.
observations.reason: Due to lack of regular employees and voluminous work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that  Management  require  the    OIC- Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their cashbook balances and subsidiary ledger balances for Cash Local Treasury (01-01-010), Cash in Bank (01-02-010/020) and Advances for Payroll at least quarterly to correct detected errors and adjust reconciling items in their respective books/records.

Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.

Require the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I

In  view  of the foregoing, we recommended that Management require coordination between the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their cashbook balances and subsidiary  ledger balances for  Cash  Local  Treasury  (01-01-010), Cash in Bank  (01-02-010/020) and Advances for Payroll at least quarterly to correct detected errors and adjust reconciling items in their respective books/records.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be required to prepare statements of bank reconciliation and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor regularly within the period prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445.  Management should also hire or assign  personnel  with Accounting Degree for the Accounting Unit so that delay in the preparation of financial statements and accounting records will be lessened.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to Management  the following:
Require the Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.
Require the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided and  see to it that PPAs are in conformity with Section1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.
Proper and regular monitoring of the implementation of GAD programs and projects so that full implementation of the same will be achieved.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements to the Audit Team.  Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.
OIC-Municipal Accountant should also make representation with the Local Chief Executive on the possibility of personnel augmentation for the Accounting Unit.

In view of the foregoing, we  recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant  to see to it that   the monthly net take home pay of an employee should not be lower than  ₱5,000.00  before issuing certification to lending institutions in strict compliance to Section 48 of  Republic  Act. No. 10924 also known as General Appropriation Act  of  2018.
Henceforth,  Management should see to it that net take home pay of employees do not fall below the minimum amount set by law.

We recommended that Management submit copies of the MOA/ Trust Agreement for the various programs/projects the Municipality had implemented thru fund transfers to support transactions/disbursements pertaining thereto and for the evaluation and validation of these projects.

We recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and  122 of P.D. No. 1445  and  COA  Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.

We recommended that the management ensure prompt and full implementation of the 20% development projects and programs during the current year in order to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated as provided under Section 287 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and  Section  5  of  DILG/DBM  Joint  Memorandum  Circular No. 2011-1  dated  April 13, 2011.

We recommended that Management fully implement priority PPAs of GAD and see to it that PPAs are in conformity with Section1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.

In  view  of  the  foregoing, we  recommended  that  Management  require  the    OIC- Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their cashbook balances and subsidiary ledger balances for Cash Local Treasury (01-01-010), Cash in Bank (01-02-010/020) and Advances for Payroll at least quarterly to correct detected errors and adjust reconciling items in their respective books/records.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.
Require the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

In view thereof, we recommended that Management submit copies of the MOA/ Trust Agreement for the various programs/projects the Municipality had implemented thru fund transfers to support transactions/disbursements pertaining thereto and for the evaluation and validation of these projects.

Likewise, Management should enhance the review mechanism in processing of Disbursement Vouchers. Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 3.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and  122 of P.D. No. 1445  and  COA  Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.

Submit to the Audit Team the documentary requirements as required under    Section 3 of  COA Circular  No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review ; and

Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply     with the Aforementioned law and  regulations  in  releasing  government  funds to   League of Municipalities  prior to the  approval  of  the  Municipal  Mayor or Municipal Vice Mayor, as the case maybe.

We recommended the immediate submission of the unsubmitted Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements as shown in Annex A. No. 7160 and Section 5 of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that Management require the LDRRMC Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the year ending December 31, 2016.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to Republic Act No. 10121 otherwise known “the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.
We recommended that Management strictly comply with to Section 21 of R.A No. 9003 on the segregation of solid wastes at source.

Require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the year ending December 31, 2015.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to RA 10121 otherwise known “the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.

The immediate submission of Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Report of Disbursements and Payrolls, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements, as well as Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts pursuant to Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1 and see to it that these provisions should be strictly complied with.

The immediate submission of the requisite supporting documents for disbursements listed in Annex A to avoid the suspension and/or disallowance of the same. Henceforth, the OIC-Municipal Accountant should ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents, prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor and see to it that disbursements are duly signed and approved by proper officials in strict compliance with the provisions of Sections 4(5) and 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their cashbook balances and subsidiary ledger balances for Cash Local Treasury (01-01-010), Cash in Bank (01-02-010/020) and Advances for Payroll at least quarterly to correct detected errors and adjust reconciling items in their respective books/records.

We recommended  the following:
Require the Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team;
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.
Require the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
Cause the immediate submission of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and the Request for Relief from Accountability for loss of property, damaged during Typhoon Yolanda; and
Conduct inventory of the totally damaged property and submit the report to the disposal committee of the Municipal Government of Ivisan as contemplated under Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445, for appropriate action.

We recommended that Management submit copies of the MOA/ Trust Agreement for the various programs/projects the Municipality had implemented thru fund transfers to support transactions/disbursements pertaining thereto and for the evaluation and validation  of these projects.  Henceforth, strictly comply  with  Sections 3.1  and 3.1.2  of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445.

Submit to the Audit Team the documentary requirements as required under
Section 3 of  COA Circular  No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review ; and

Aforementioned law  and  regulations  in  releasing  government  funds   to
League of Municipalities  prior to the  approval  of  the  Municipal  Mayor or

We therefore recommended the following:
Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other    emoluments through bank;
Make a representation with any government bank for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and
Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended the immediate submission of the unsubmitted Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements as shown in Annex A. We recommended that the Management ensure prompt and full implementation of the 20% development projects and programs  during the current year in order to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated as provided under Section 287 of  R.A. No. 7160 and Section 5 of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that Management require the LDRRMC Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the year ending December 31, 2016.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to Republic Act No. 10121 otherwise known “the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.
We recommended that Management strictly comply with to Section 21 of R.A No. 9003 on the segregation of solid wastes at source.

We recommended the following:
Cause the monitoring of GAD activities and constant reconciliation with accounting records to come up with a reliable GAD report;
Submit a copy of the corresponding annual GAD accomplishment report to the  Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year; and
Henceforth, comply strictly with Sections 4.8 and 5.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 of DBM, NEDA, and NCRFW.
